title: ExtrudeFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing extrude features in a design and supports the ability to create new extrude features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new extrude feature based on the information defined by the provided ExtrudeFeatureInput object. To create a new extrusion use the createInput function to create a new input object and use the methods and properties on that object to define the required input for an extrusion. Once the information is defined on the input object you can pass it to the Add method to create the extrusion.

methods.name: addSimple
methods.description: Creates a basic extrusion that goes from the profile plane the specified distance.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a new ExtrudeFeatureInput object that is used to specify the input needed to create a new extrude feature.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified extrude feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified extrude feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of extrude features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Move Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new move feature.

related_samples.name: Manage Participant Bodies API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to manage participant During Cut operation. Same API can be used to manage participants During intersection also.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.name: ReplaceFace Feature
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new replaceface feature.

related_samples.name: Shell Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new shell feature.

related_samples.name: Simple Extrusion Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

add.title: ExtrudeFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new extrude feature based on the information defined by the provided ExtrudeFeatureInput object. To create a new extrusion use the createInput function to create a new input object and use the methods and properties on that object to define the required input for an extrusion. Once the information is defined on the input object you can pass it to the Add method to create the extrusion.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created ExtrudeFeature or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: The ExtrudeFeatureInput object that specifies the input needed to create a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: Extrude Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setOneSideExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setOneSideExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setSymmetricExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setSymmetricExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using thin extrude
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setThinExtrude method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a sketch with a profile. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the thin extrusion.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using ThroughAllExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the ThroughAllExtent method.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.add using setTwoSidesExtent
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.add method using the setTwoSidesExtent method. To use this sample have a design open that contains a profile and a body that is positioned away from the profile but is a size where when the profile is extruded it will intersect the body. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion and the body to extrude to. The extrusion will be created up to the body with an offset and will also be offset from the sketch plane.

related_samples.name: Manage Participant Bodies API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to manage participant During Cut operation. Same API can be used to manage participants During intersection also.

addSimple.title: ExtrudeFeatures.addSimple Method
addSimple.description: Creates a basic extrusion that goes from the profile plane the specified distance.
addSimple.return_description: Returns the newly created ExtrudeFeature or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: The profile argument can be a single Profile, a single planar face, a single SketchText object, or an ObjectCollection consisting of multiple profiles, planar faces, and sketch texts. When an ObjectCollection is used all of the profiles, faces, and sketch texts must be co-planar.   This method can only be used to create solid extrusions. To create a surface extrusion you need to use the add method.

parameters.name: distance
parameters.description: ValueInput object that defines the extrude distance. A positive value extrudes in the positive direction of the sketch plane and negative value is in the opposite direction.

related_samples.name: extrudeFeatures.addSimple
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the extrudeFeatures.addSimple method.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new extrude feature.

related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to manage participant During Cut operation. Same API can be used to manage participants During intersection also.

related_samples.name: Patch Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new patch feature.

related_samples.description: Creates a new extrusion feature, resulting in a new component.

classType.title: ExtrudeFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: ExtrudeFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a new ExtrudeFeatureInput object that is used to specify the input needed to create a new extrude feature.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created ExtrudeFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: profile
parameters.description: The profile argument can be a single Profile, a single planar face, a single SketchText object, or an ObjectCollection consisting of multiple profiles, planar faces, and sketch texts. When an ObjectCollection is used all of the profiles, faces, and sketch texts must be co-planar.   To create a surface (non-solid) extrusion, you can use the createOpenProfile and createBRepEdgeProfile methods of the Component object to create an open profile. You also need to set the isSolid property of the returned ExtrudeFeatureInput property to False.

To use this sample have a design open that contains a profile and a body that is positioned away from the profile but is a size where when the profile is extruded it will intersect the body. When you run the script you will be prompted to select the profile that will be used to create the extrusion and the body to extrude to. The extrusion will be created up to the body with an offset and will also be offset from the sketch plane.

item.title: ExtrudeFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified extrude feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: ExtrudeFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified extrude feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: ExtrudeFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of extrude features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = extrudeFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: ExtrudeFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = extrudeFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
